verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est: cum esset in quodam loco orans, ut cessavit, dixit unus ex discipulis ejus ad eum: Domine, doce nos orare, sicut docuit et Joannes discipulos suos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν τῷ εἶναι αὐτὸν ἐν τόπῳ τινὶ προσευχόμενον ὡς ἐπαύσατο εἶπέν τις τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ πρὸς αὐτόν κύριε δίδαξον ἡμᾶς προσεύχεσθαι καθὼς καὶ ἰωάννης ἐδίδαξεν τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that, as he was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him, Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Cum oratis, dicite: Pater, sanctificetur nomen tuum. Adveniat regnum tuum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτοῖς ὅταν προσεύχησθε λέγετε πάτερ ἡμῶν ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοις, ἁγιασθήτω τὸ ὄνομά σου ἐλθέτω ἡ βασιλεία σου γενηθήτω τὸ θέλημά σου ὡς ἐν οὐρανῳ, καὶ ἐπὶ τὴς γὴς
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, When ye pray, say, Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Panem nostrum quotidianum da nobis hodie.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν ἄρτον ἡμῶν τὸν ἐπιούσιον δίδου ἡμῖν τὸ καθ᾽ ἡμέραν
verses.king_james: Give us day by day our daily bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dimitte nobis peccata nostra, siquidem et ipsi dimittimus omni debenti nobis. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἄφες ἡμῖν τὰς ἁμαρτίας ἡμῶν καὶ γὰρ αὐτοὶ ἀφίεμεν παντὶ ὀφείλοντι ἡμῖν καὶ μὴ εἰσενέγκῃς ἡμᾶς εἰς πειρασμόν ἀλλὰ ῥῦσαι ἡμᾶς ἀπὸ τοῦ πονηροῦ
verses.king_james: And forgive us our sins; for we also forgive every one that is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation; but deliver us from evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ad illos: Quis vestrum habebit amicum, et ibit ad illum media nocte, et dicet illi: Amice, commoda mihi tres panes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς τίς ἐξ ὑμῶν ἕξει φίλον καὶ πορεύσεται πρὸς αὐτὸν μεσονυκτίου καὶ εἴπῃ αὐτῷ φίλε χρῆσόν μοι τρεῖς ἄρτους
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say unto him, Friend, lend me three loaves;

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam amicus meus venit de via ad me, et non habeo quod ponam ante illum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπειδὴ φίλος μου παρεγένετο ἐξ ὁδοῦ πρός με καὶ οὐκ ἔχω ὃ παραθήσω αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: For a friend of mine in his journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him?

verses.latin_vulgate: et ille de intus respondens dicat: Noli mihi molestus esse, jam ostium clausum est, et pueri mei mecum sunt in cubili: non possum surgere, et dare tibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀκεῖνος ἔσωθεν ἀποκριθεὶς εἴπῃ μή μοι κόπους πάρεχε ἤδη ἡ θύρα κέκλεισται καὶ τὰ παιδία μου μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ εἰς τὴν κοίτην εἰσίν οὐ δύναμαι ἀναστὰς δοῦναί σοι
verses.king_james: And he from within shall answer and say, Trouble me not: the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si ille perseveraverit pulsans: dico vobis, etsi non dabit illi surgens eo quod amicus ejus sit, propter improbitatem tamen ejus surget, et dabit illi quotquot habet necessarios.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω ὑμῖν εἰ καὶ οὐ δώσει αὐτῷ ἀναστὰς διὰ τὸ εἶναι αὐτοῦ φίλον διά γε τὴν ἀναίδειαν αὐτοῦ ἐγερθεὶς δώσει αὐτῷ ὅσων χρῄζει
verses.king_james: I say unto you, Though he will not rise and give him, because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego dico vobis: Petite, et dabitur vobis; quærite, et invenietis; pulsate, et aperietur vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ ὑμῖν λέγω αἰτεῖτε καὶ δοθήσεται ὑμῖν ζητεῖτε καὶ εὑρήσετε κρούετε καὶ ἀνοιγήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: And I say unto you, Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis enim qui petit, accipit: et qui quærit, invenit: et pulsanti aperietur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς γὰρ ὁ αἰτῶν λαμβάνει καὶ ὁ ζητῶν εὑρίσκει καὶ τῷ κρούοντι ἀνοιγήσεται
verses.king_james: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis autem ex vobis patrem petit panem, numquid lapidem dabit illi? aut piscem, numquid pro pisce serpentem dabit illi?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίνα δὲ ὑμῶν τὸν πατέρα αἰτήσει ὁ υἱὸς ἄρτον, μὴ λὶθον ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ εἰ καὶ ἰχθύν μὴ ἀντὶ ἰχθύος ὄφιν ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if *he ask* a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut si petierit ovum, numquid porriget illi scorpionem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ καὶ ἐὰν αἰτήσῃ ᾠόν μὴ ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ σκορπίον
verses.king_james: Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo vos, cum sitis mali, nostis bona data dare filiis vestris: quanto magis Pater vester de cælo dabit spiritum bonum petentibus se?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὖν ὑμεῖς πονηροὶ ὑπάρχοντες οἴδατε ἀγαθὰ δόματα διδόναι τοῖς τέκνοις ὑμῶν πόσῳ μᾶλλον ὁ πατὴρ ὁ ἐξ οὐρανοῦ δώσει πνεῦμα ἅγιον τοῖς αἰτοῦσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall *your* heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat ejiciens dæmonium, et illud erat mutum. Et cum ejecisset dæmonium, locutus est mutus, et admiratæ sunt turbæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν ἐκβάλλων δαιμόνιον καὶ αὐτὸ ἦν κωφόν ἐγένετο δὲ τοῦ δαιμονίου ἐξελθόντος ἐλάλησεν ὁ κωφός καὶ ἐθαύμασαν οἱ ὄχλοι
verses.king_james: And he was casting out a devil, and it was dumb. And it came to pass, when the devil was gone out, the dumb spake; and the people wondered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam autem ex eis dixerunt: In Beelzebub principe dæmoniorum ejicit dæmonia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τινὲς δὲ ἐξ αὐτῶν εἶπον ἐν βεελζεβοὺλ ἄρχοντι τῶν δαιμονίων ἐκβάλλει τὰ δαιμόνια
verses.king_james: But some of them said, He casteth out devils through Beelzebub the chief of the devils.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alii tentantes, signum de cælo quærebant ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕτεροι δὲ πειράζοντες σημεῖον παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ ἐζήτουν ἐξ οὐρανοῦ
verses.king_james: And others, tempting *him*, sought of him a sign from heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse autem ut vidit cogitationes eorum, dixit eis: Omne regnum in seipsum divisum desolabitur, et domus supra domum cadet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτὸς δὲ εἰδὼς αὐτῶν τὰ διανοήματα εἶπεν αὐτοῖς πᾶσα βασιλεία ἐφ᾽ ἑαυτὴν διαμερισθεῖσα ἐρημοῦται καὶ οἶκος ἐπὶ οἶκον πίπτει
verses.king_james: But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house *divided* against a house falleth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem et Satanas in seipsum divisus est, quomodo stabit regnum ejus? quia dicitis in Beelzebub me ejicere dæmonia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ καὶ ὁ σατανᾶς ἐφ᾽ ἑαυτὸν διεμερίσθη πῶς σταθήσεται ἡ βασιλεία αὐτοῦ ὅτι λέγετε ἐν βεελζεβοὺλ ἐκβάλλειν με τὰ δαιμόνια
verses.king_james: If Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out devils through Beelzebub.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem ego in Beelzebub ejicio dæmonia: filii vestri in quo ejiciunt? ideo ipsi judices vestri erunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ἐγὼ ἐν βεελζεβοὺλ ἐκβάλλω τὰ δαιμόνια οἱ υἱοὶ ὑμῶν ἐν τίνι ἐκβάλλουσιν διὰ τοῦτο κριταὶ ὑμῶν αὐτοὶ ἔσονται
verses.king_james: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast *them* out? therefore shall they be your judges.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro si in digito Dei ejicio dæmonia: profecto pervenit in vos regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ἐν δακτύλῳ θεοῦ ἐκβάλλω τὰ δαιμόνια ἄρα ἔφθασεν ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum fortis armatus custodit atrium suum, in pace sunt ea quæ possidet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν ὁ ἰσχυρὸς καθωπλισμένος φυλάσσῃ τὴν ἑαυτοῦ αὐλήν ἐν εἰρήνῃ ἐστὶν τὰ ὑπάρχοντα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem fortior eo superveniens vicerit eum, universa arma ejus auferet, in quibus confidebat, et spolia ejus distribuet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπὰν δὲ ὁ ἰσχυρότερος αὐτοῦ ἐπελθὼν νικήσῃ αὐτόν τὴν πανοπλίαν αὐτοῦ αἴρει ἐφ᾽ ᾗ ἐπεποίθει καὶ τὰ σκῦλα αὐτοῦ διαδίδωσιν
verses.king_james: But when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui non est mecum, contra me est: et qui non colligit mecum, dispergit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μὴ ὢν μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ κατ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐστιν καὶ ὁ μὴ συνάγων μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ σκορπίζει
verses.king_james: He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum immundus spiritus exierit de homine, ambulat per loca inaquosa, quærens requiem: et non inveniens dicit: Revertar in domum meam unde exivi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν τὸ ἀκάθαρτον πνεῦμα ἐξέλθῃ ἀπὸ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου διέρχεται δι᾽ ἀνύδρων τόπων ζητοῦν ἀνάπαυσιν καὶ μὴ εὑρίσκον λέγει ὑποστρέψω εἰς τὸν οἶκόν μου ὅθεν ἐξῆλθον
verses.king_james: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest; and finding none, he saith, I will return unto my house whence I came out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum venerit, invenit eam scopis mundatam, et ornatam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὸν εὑρίσκει σεσαρωμένον καὶ κεκοσμημένον
verses.king_james: And when he cometh, he findeth *it* swept and garnished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc vadit, et assumit septem alios spiritus secum, nequiores se, et ingressi habitant ibi. Et fiunt novissima hominis illius pejora prioribus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε πορεύεται καὶ παραλαμβάνει ἑπτά ἕτερα πνεύματα πονηρότερα ἑαυτοῦ καὶ εἰσελθόντα κατοικεῖ ἐκεῖ καὶ γίνεται τὰ ἔσχατα τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκείνου χείρονα τῶν πρώτων
verses.king_james: Then goeth he, and taketh *to him* seven other spirits more wicked than himself; and they enter in, and dwell there: and the last *state* of that man is worse than the first.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, cum hæc diceret: extollens vocem quædam mulier de turba dixit illi: Beatus venter qui te portavit, et ubera quæ suxisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν τῷ λέγειν αὐτὸν ταῦτα ἐπάρασά τις γυνὴ φωνὴν ἐκ τοῦ ὄχλου εἶπεν αὐτῷ μακαρία ἡ κοιλία ἡ βαστάσασά σε καὶ μαστοὶ οὓς ἐθήλασας
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as he spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed *is* the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille dixit: Quinimmo beati, qui audiunt verbum Dei et custodiunt illud.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτὸς δὲ εἶπεν μενοῦνγε μακάριοι οἱ ἀκούοντες τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ φυλάσσοντες αὐτὸν
verses.king_james: But he said, Yea rather, blessed *are* they that hear the word of God, and keep it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Turbis autem concurrentibus cœpit dicere: Generatio hæc, generatio nequam est: signum quærit, et signum non dabitur ei, nisi signum Jonæ prophetæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῶν δὲ ὄχλων ἐπαθροιζομένων ἤρξατο λέγειν ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη πονηρά ἐστιν σημεῖον ἐπιζητεῖ, καὶ σημεῖον οὐ δοθήσεται αὐτῇ εἰ μὴ τὸ σημεῖον ἰωνᾶ τοῦ προφήτου
verses.king_james: And when the people were gathered thick together, he began to say, This is an evil generation: they seek a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam sicut fuit Jonas signum Ninivitis, ita erit et Filius hominis generationi isti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γὰρ ἐγένετο ἰωνᾶς σημεῖον τοῖς νινευίταις οὕτως ἔσται καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τῇ γενεᾷ ταύτῃ
verses.king_james: For as Jonas was a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Regina austri surget in judicio cum viris generationis hujus, et condemnabit illos: quia venit a finibus terræ audire sapientiam Salomonis: et ecce plus quam Salomon hic.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βασίλισσα νότου ἐγερθήσεται ἐν τῇ κρίσει μετὰ τῶν ἀνδρῶν τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης καὶ κατακρινεῖ αὐτούς ὅτι ἦλθεν ἐκ τῶν περάτων τῆς γῆς ἀκοῦσαι τὴν σοφίαν σολομῶντος, καὶ ἰδού, πλεῖον σολομῶντος ὧδε
verses.king_james: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them: for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon *is* here.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viri Ninivitæ surgent in judicio cum generatione hac, et condemnabunt illam: quia pœnitentiam egerunt ad prædicationem Jonæ, et ecce plus quam Jonas hic.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄνδρες νινευῒ ἀναστήσονται ἐν τῇ κρίσει μετὰ τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης καὶ κατακρινοῦσιν αὐτήν ὅτι μετενόησαν εἰς τὸ κήρυγμα ἰωνᾶ καὶ ἰδού, πλεῖον ἰωνᾶ ὧδε
verses.king_james: The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas *is* here.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo lucernam accendit, et in abscondito ponit, neque sub modio: sed supra candelabrum, ut qui ingrediuntur, lumen videant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς δὲ λύχνον ἅψας εἰς κρυπτόν τίθησιν οὐδὲ ὑπὸ τὸν μόδιον ἀλλ᾽ ἐπὶ τὴν λυχνίαν ἵνα οἱ εἰσπορευόμενοι τὸ φέγγος βλέπωσιν
verses.king_james: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, putteth *it* in a secret place, neither under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that they which come in may see the light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lucerna corporis tui est oculus tuus. Si oculus tuus fuerit simplex, totum corpus tuum lucidum erit: si autem nequam fuerit, etiam corpus tuum tenebrosum erit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ λύχνος τοῦ σώματός ἐστιν ὁ ὀφθαλμός ὅταν οὖν ὁ ὀφθαλμός σου ἁπλοῦς ᾖ καὶ ὅλον τὸ σῶμά σου φωτεινόν ἐστιν ἐπὰν δὲ πονηρὸς ᾖ καὶ τὸ σῶμά σου σκοτεινόν
verses.king_james: The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when *thine eye* is evil, thy body also *is* full of darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vide ergo ne lumen quod in te est, tenebræ sint.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σκόπει οὖν μὴ τὸ φῶς τὸ ἐν σοὶ σκότος ἐστίν
verses.king_james: Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo corpus tuum totum lucidum fuerit, non habens aliquam partem tenebrarum, erit lucidum totum, et sicut lucerna fulgoris illuminabit te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὖν τὸ σῶμά σου ὅλον φωτεινόν μὴ ἔχον τι μέρος σκοτεινόν ἔσται φωτεινὸν ὅλον ὡς ὅταν ὁ λύχνος τῇ ἀστραπῇ φωτίζῃ σε
verses.king_james: If thy whole body therefore *be* full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum loqueretur, rogavit illum quidam pharisæus ut pranderet apud se. Et ingressus recubuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῷ λαλῆσαι ἠρωτᾷ αὐτὸν φαρισαῖος τις ὅπως ἀριστήσῃ παρ᾽ αὐτῷ εἰσελθὼν δὲ ἀνέπεσεν
verses.king_james: And as he spake, a certain Pharisee besought him to dine with him: and he went in, and sat down to meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæus autem cœpit intra se reputans dicere, quare non baptizatus esset ante prandium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ φαρισαῖος ἰδὼν ἐθαύμασεν ὅτι οὐ πρῶτον ἐβαπτίσθη πρὸ τοῦ ἀρίστου
verses.king_james: And when the Pharisee saw *it*, he marvelled that he had not first washed before dinner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Dominus ad illum: Nunc vos pharisæi, quod deforis est calicis et catini, mundatis: quod autem intus est vestrum, plenum est rapina et iniquitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ κύριος πρὸς αὐτόν νῦν ὑμεῖς οἱ φαρισαῖοι τὸ ἔξωθεν τοῦ ποτηρίου καὶ τοῦ πίνακος καθαρίζετε τὸ δὲ ἔσωθεν ὑμῶν γέμει ἁρπαγῆς καὶ πονηρίας
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto him, Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stulti! nonne qui fecit quod deforis est, etiam id quod deintus est fecit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄφρονες οὐχ ὁ ποιήσας τὸ ἔξωθεν καὶ τὸ ἔσωθεν ἐποίησεν
verses.king_james: *Ye* fools, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen quod superest, date eleemosynam: et ecce omnia munda sunt vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν τὰ ἐνόντα δότε ἐλεημοσύνην καὶ ἰδού, πάντα καθαρὰ ὑμῖν ἐστιν
verses.king_james: But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed væ vobis, pharisæis, quia decimatis mentham, et rutam, et omne olus, et præteritis judicium et caritatem Dei: hæc autem oportuit facere, et illa non omittere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ οὐαὶ ὑμῖν τοῖς φαρισαίοις ὅτι ἀποδεκατοῦτε τὸ ἡδύοσμον καὶ τὸ πήγανον καὶ πᾶν λάχανον καὶ παρέρχεσθε τὴν κρίσιν καὶ τὴν ἀγάπην τοῦ θεοῦ ταῦτα ἔδει ποιῆσαι κἀκεῖνα μὴ ἀφιέναι
verses.king_james: But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ vobis, pharisæis, quia diligitis primas cathedras in synagogis, et salutationes in foro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ ὑμῖν τοῖς φαρισαίοις ὅτι ἀγαπᾶτε τὴν πρωτοκαθεδρίαν ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς καὶ τοὺς ἀσπασμοὺς ἐν ταῖς ἀγοραῖς
verses.king_james: Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ vobis, quia estis ut monumenta, quæ non apparent, et homines ambulantes supra, nesciunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ ὑμῖν γραμματεῖς καὶ φαρισαῖοι, ὑποκριταί, ὅτι ἐστὲ ὡς τὰ μνημεῖα τὰ ἄδηλα καὶ οἱ ἄνθρωποι οἱ περιπατοῦντες ἐπάνω οὐκ οἴδασιν
verses.king_james: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are as graves which appear not, and the men that walk over *them* are not aware *of them*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem quidam ex legisperitis, ait illi: Magister, hæc dicens etiam contumeliam nobis facis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δέ τις τῶν νομικῶν λέγει αὐτῷ διδάσκαλε ταῦτα λέγων καὶ ἡμᾶς ὑβρίζεις
verses.king_james: Then answered one of the lawyers, and said unto him, Master, thus saying thou reproachest us also.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille ait: Et vobis legisperitis væ: quia oneratis homines oneribus, quæ portare non possunt, et ipsi uno digito vestro non tangitis sarcinas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν καὶ ὑμῖν τοῖς νομικοῖς οὐαί ὅτι φορτίζετε τοὺς ἀνθρώπους φορτία δυσβάστακτα καὶ αὐτοὶ ἑνὶ τῶν δακτύλων ὑμῶν οὐ προσψαύετε τοῖς φορτίοις
verses.king_james: And he said, Woe unto you also, *ye* lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ vobis, qui ædificatis monumenta prophetarum: patres autem vestri occiderunt illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ ὑμῖν ὅτι οἰκοδομεῖτε τὰ μνημεῖα τῶν προφητῶν οἱ δὲ πατέρες ὑμῶν ἀπέκτειναν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: Woe unto you! for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Profecto testificamini quod consentitis operibus patrum vestrorum: quoniam ipsi quidem eos occiderunt, vos autem ædificatis eorum sepulchra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα μάρτυρεῖτε καὶ συνευδοκεῖτε τοῖς ἔργοις τῶν πατέρων ὑμῶν ὅτι αὐτοὶ μὲν ἀπέκτειναν αὐτοὺς ὑμεῖς δὲ οἰκοδομεῖτε αὐτῶν τὰ μνημεῖα
verses.king_james: Truly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchres.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea et sapientia Dei dixit: Mittam ad illos prophetas, et apostolos, et ex illis occident, et persequentur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο καὶ ἡ σοφία τοῦ θεοῦ εἶπεν ἀποστελῶ εἰς αὐτοὺς προφήτας καὶ ἀποστόλους καὶ ἐξ αὐτῶν ἀποκτενοῦσιν καὶ ἐκδιώξουσιν
verses.king_james: Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and *some* of them they shall slay and persecute:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut inquiratur sanguis omnium prophetarum, qui effusus est a constitutione mundi a generatione ista,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ἐκζητηθῇ τὸ αἷμα πάντων τῶν προφητῶν τὸ ἐκχυνόμενον ἀπὸ καταβολῆς κόσμου ἀπὸ τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης
verses.king_james: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;

verses.latin_vulgate: a sanguine Abel, usque ad sanguinem Zachariæ, qui periit inter altare et ædem. Ita dico vobis, requiretur ab hac generatione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος ἅβελ ἕως τοῦ αἵματος ξαχαρίου τοῦ ἀπολομένου μεταξὺ τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου καὶ τοῦ οἴκου ναί λέγω ὑμῖν ἐκζητηθήσεται ἀπὸ τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης
verses.king_james: From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ vobis, legisperitis, quia tulistis clavem scientiæ: ipsi non introistis, et eos qui introibant, prohibuistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ ὑμῖν τοῖς νομικοῖς ὅτι ἤρατε τὴν κλεῖδα τῆς γνώσεως αὐτοὶ οὐκ εἰσήλθετε, καὶ τοὺς εἰσερχομένους ἐκωλύσατε
verses.king_james: Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem hæc ad illos diceret, cœperunt pharisæi et legisperiti graviter insistere, et os ejus opprimere de multis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντος δὲ αὐτοῦ ταῦτα πρὸς αὐτοὺς, ἤρξαντο οἱ γραμματεῖς καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι δεινῶς ἐνέχειν καὶ ἀποστοματίζειν αὐτὸν περὶ πλειόνων
verses.king_james: And as he said these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to urge *him* vehemently, and to provoke him to speak of many things:

verses.latin_vulgate: insidiantes ei, et quærentes aliquid capere de ore ejus, ut accusarent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐνεδρεύοντες αὐτὸν καὶ ζητοὺντες θηρεῦσαί τι ἐκ τοῦ στόματος αὐτοῦ ἵνα κατηγορήσωσιν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.
